quote: The cravings don't disappear. You just get faster at remembering why you stopped.
Some days you hold it together so well you forget you're still broken.
Falling apart in private still counts as falling apart. You're allowed to say so.
Being too much was never the problem. You were just always in the wrong rooms.
Somewhere between exhausted and asleep, you admitted you haven't been fine for a while.
Tired isn't even the right word anymore. Tired left months ago.
Asking for help is the part they never tell you is brave.
Nobody tells you that getting better feels like grief too.
The heaviest thing you carry is knowing exactly what's wrong and still not being able to explain it to anyone.
Putting yourself last is not the same as being selfless. It just hurts more quietly.
Not being able to explain the pain doesn't mean the pain isn't real. It means it's older than your words for it.
Staying busy was never a coping mechanism. It was just a way to avoid the quiet long enough to fall asleep.
Some nights the loneliness isn't about being alone. It's about being surrounded and still invisible.
Letting someone in after keeping everyone out is one of the bravest things a body can do.
Nobody warns you that the version of yourself you're saving is someone you haven't met yet.
The version of you that keeps everyone else calm is still allowed to fall apart.
Grief doesn't need a reason you can defend. Losing yourself counts.
Falling apart slowly isn't weakness. It's what happens when you've been strong for too long with no one noticing.
